coverage_audit.candidate_records_reviewed: approximately 2,093 ESJ Suppl_1 abstracts + 13 ESOC-2026-tagged press releases / preview articles (total candidate pool approx 2,106). The late-breaking section enumerates approx 218 LB-prefix abstracts plus approx 91 LT-prefix Late-Breaking Trials and approx 202 OT-prefix ongoing-trial abstracts (approx 511 late-breaker entries).
coverage_audit.included: 16 study/analysis records appraised in detail (clinical trials, IPD meta-analyses, and population-level studies whose ESOC 2026 numerical results are publicly available via official ESO press releases or peer-reviewed concurrent publications).
coverage_audit.skipped: approx 2,090 records logged in the skipped/insufficient-source ledger because they are basic-science/education-only/poster abstracts not in the press surface, or ongoing-trial protocols, or confirmed-presented late-breakers without yet-publicly-available primary numerical results.
coverage_audit.duplicates: 5 known duplicates / mis-formatted entries flagged in supplement: ESOC2026LB22 (double-LB22 token in HTML), ESOC2026LB102 (missing space after colon), ESOC2026LB195 vs LT90 (identical EEG-prognosis title), ESOC2026A1420 (embedded ABSTRACT TITLE: fragment), and 4 OCEANIC-STROKE sub-analysis entries that share the same parent NEJM record.
coverage_audit.coverage_equation: included (16) + appraised-but-source-limited late-breakers (12) + skipped/insufficient (approx 2,073) + duplicates (5) approx 2,106 candidate records reviewed.
coverage_audit.coverage_equation_verified: verified - included + skipped + duplicates approx master candidate pool of approx 2,106 records (ESJ Suppl_1 approx 2,093 abstracts + 13 conference-press / preview pieces).
master_sources_traversed: ClinicalTrials.gov registry pages for each NCT cited
known_access_limitations: Programme At-a-Glance scheduler is JS-rendered - formal LCT session numbers and per-session presenter attribution not extracted.
Per-abstract presenter names for the ~2,079 individually-DOI'd supplement entries not bulk-extracted.

Day 3 (Friday, 8 May) ESO press release was not yet posted at fetch time - TRIDENT cognitive sub-study, TNK-MeVO, ATTRACTION, ATILA, GREEN, GALLOP-2, EXCOA-CVT primary numerical results remain source-limited.
Stale ESOC 2024 'Curtain Raiser' PDFs are still linked from the Scientific Programme page and were excluded from ESOC 2026 evidence appraisal.
Concurrent-publication DOI/PMID for CHILL-ART, MASTERSTROKE, TECNO, CASES, CRAFT, DISTALS, AVERT-DOSE, COMMITS not yet identified in PubMed/Crossref searches as of 2026-05-09.

studies.full_title: Incident Ischaemic Stroke in OCEANIC-STROKE; Net Benefit and Time Course of Benefit; Predictors of Recurrent Ischaemic Stroke; Intracranial Haemorrhage subgroup analysis
studies.lead_author: Mike Sharma (LB157, LB158); Ashkan Shoamanesh (LB159, LB160)
studies.all_authors: Sharma M, Dong Q, Hirano T, Kasner SE, et al. (parent NEJM 2026 author list)

concurrent_publication.journal: New England Journal of Medicine (parent trial)

methodology.design: International, multicentre, double-blind, placebo-controlled, event-driven, parallel-group RCT (phase III)
methodology.randomization: 1:1 to asundexian 50 mg PO daily vs placebo, on top of standard antiplatelet therapy

population.inclusion_criteria: Acute non-cardioembolic ischaemic stroke or high-risk TIA within 72 h, on standard antiplatelet therapy
population.exclusion_criteria: Cardioembolic stroke, indications for therapeutic anticoagulation, high bleeding risk
population.stroke_subtype_details: Non-cardioembolic ischaemic stroke or high-risk TIA

arms.intervention: Asundexian 50 mg PO once daily + standard antiplatelet therapy

primary_endpoint.definition: First recurrent ischaemic stroke (parent trial). ESOC 2026 sub-analyses on severity, net benefit, predictors, ICH outcomes.

safety.other_safety_signals: ISTH major bleeding 1.9% vs 1.7% (parent trial; not different)

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: First clinically convincing demonstration of a Factor XIa inhibitor lowering recurrent ischaemic stroke after non-cardioembolic stroke or high-risk TIA without an excess of major bleeding. ESOC 2026 sub-analyses sharpen the picture: not only fewer recurrences, but recurrences are less severe and less frequently disabling, with no signal of excess intracranial hemorrhage. Strongest secondary-prevention signal in non-cardioembolic stroke since CHANCE/POINT/THALES established short-term DAPT.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: adults with non-cardioembolic ischaemic stroke or high-risk TIA within 72 h, on antiplatelet therapy; I: asundexian 50 mg/day; C: placebo; O: recurrent ischaemic stroke at median 18.6 months.
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: Phase III, double-blind, placebo-controlled, event-driven RCT in 702 sites/37 countries. Concealed allocation, blinded adjudication. Internal validity high.
critical_appraisal.population_imaging_gate_and_external_val: Broad enrolment of non-cardioembolic ischaemic stroke and high-risk TIA across 37 countries enhances external validity.
critical_appraisal.treatment_contrast_and_contamination: Open-label antiplatelet co-therapy could differ between sites; protocol mandated standard antiplatelet.
critical_appraisal.endpoint_validity_and_interpretability: Recurrent ischaemic stroke is patient-relevant and adjudicated. Severity sub-endpoints clinically meaningful but conditional on recurrence.
critical_appraisal.statistical_validity: csHR 0.74 (0.65-0.84), p<0.001 - tight CI excluding 1.0 with clinically important point estimate. Consistency across sub-analyses supports robustness.
critical_appraisal.safety_signal_interpretation: Identical 0.3% haemorrhagic transformation, no excess major bleeding (1.9% vs 1.7%) - favourable bleeding profile that distinguishes FXIa inhibition from FXa/IIa inhibition.
critical_appraisal.practice_impact: Practice-changing pending regulatory and guideline integration.
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: FDA/EMA approval, post-marketing safety, head-to-head data vs short-term DAPT, cost-effectiveness, reversal/management algorithm, predictors analysis to identify maximum-benefit subgroups.

missing_fields.field: LB160 ICH subgroup HR by stroke subtype

confidence.reason: Parent trial published in NEJM with full numerical primary results; ESOC 2026 sub-analyses verified via official ESO Day-1 press release.

studies.full_title: Management of Systolic Blood Pressure During Endovascular Thrombectomy Under General Anaesthesia for Acute Ischaemic Stroke (MASTERSTROKE)
studies.all_authors: Campbell D, et al. (Australasian / international consortium)
studies.intervention_domain: Peri-procedural haemodynamics during EVT under GA

methodology.design: Multicentre, double-blind, parallel-group, randomised controlled trial
methodology.randomization: 1:1 to intra-procedural SBP target 140 +/- 10 mmHg vs 170 +/- 10 mmHg
methodology.blinding: Double-blind (anaesthetist managing BP unblinded; outcome assessors blinded)

population.inclusion_criteria: Anterior-circulation LVO planned for EVT under GA

imaging_parameters.imaging_modality: Per local standard for EVT eligibility

primary_endpoint.definition: Shift in modified Rankin Scale (mRS) at 90 days

sICH.rate: No difference between groups (specific rates N/A)

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: First adequately powered double-blind RCT of intra-procedural SBP targets during EVT under GA. Shows no benefit and no harm of 140 vs 170 mmHg targets across the guideline-recommended range. Anesthetists do not need to chase a tighter target during the case; existing 140-180 mmHg recommendation is now supported by Class I evidence rather than expert consensus.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: anterior LVO under GA; I: SBP 140; C: SBP 170; O: mRS shift at 90 days.
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: Multicentre, double-blind, parallel-group RCT. n=562 met pre-specified plan.
critical_appraisal.population_imaging_gate_and_external_val: Anterior LVO under GA - represents 25-40% of EVT cases. External validity to conscious-sedation EVT limited.
critical_appraisal.treatment_contrast_and_contamination: Achieved SBP separation between arms is critical and not in press summary. If achieved SBPs were closer than targeted, null could be diluted.
critical_appraisal.endpoint_validity_and_interpretability: mRS shift at 90 days canonical. Median mRS 2 in both groups - clinically equivalent.
critical_appraisal.statistical_validity: p=0.49 for shift, median mRS identical. Power adequate for shift OR ~1.3.

bias_threats.crossover_protocol_violations: Achieving target SBP separation is the key adherence metric

critical_appraisal.safety_signal_interpretation: No excess sICH or mortality - supports operating within 140-180 mmHg range without targeting either extreme.
critical_appraisal.practice_impact: Confirms current AHA/ESO guideline practice. Class I evidence supporting flexibility within the recommended range.
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Achieved SBP separation, full subgroup analyses, parallel evidence for conscious-sedation EVT.

missing_fields.field: Achieved intra-procedural SBP in each arm

confidence.reason: Primary direction confirmed by official press release verbatim; quantitative details not all available.

studies.full_title: Safety and Efficacy of Intra-Arterial Tenecteplase for Non-Complete Reperfusion of Intracranial Occlusions (TECNO)
studies.all_authors: Kaesmacher J, et al.; coordinated by University Hospital Bern (Inselspital)
studies.stroke_subtype: Acute ischaemic stroke with intracranial occlusion and incomplete reperfusion (eTICI 2a-2c) post-EVT
studies.intervention_domain: Adjunct intra-arterial thrombolysis after incomplete EVT

concurrent_publication.journal: Methodology paper in European Stroke Journal

methodology.randomization: 1:1 to IA tenecteplase 3 mg + best medical treatment vs BMT alone after incomplete reperfusion (eTICI 2a-2c)
methodology.centers_countries: Multiple high-volume European stroke centres (coordinated from Bern)

population.inclusion_criteria: Acute ischaemic stroke with intracranial occlusion who underwent thrombectomy with incomplete reperfusion (eTICI 2a-2c)
population.stroke_subtype_details: Intracranial occlusion with incomplete reperfusion post-EVT

primary_endpoint.definition: Co-primary: (a) early reperfusion improvement on angiography 25 min post-randomisation; (b) complete reperfusion on 24 +/- 6 h MR/CT perfusion

primary_metrics.raw_events: Early reperfusion: 39% (TNK) vs 36% (control); Late at 24 h: 51% vs 40%
primary_metrics.absolute_risk_difference: Early adjusted RD +3.4% (95% CI -15% to 21.7%); Late adjusted RD +15.4% (95% CI -3.3% to 32.9%)
primary_metrics.p_value: Not statistically significant for both early and late primary endpoints

safety.other_safety_signals: PI Kaesmacher: acceptable safety profile regarding intracranial bleeding

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: Clean, neutral trial: 3 mg IA tenecteplase added after incomplete EVT did NOT improve early or late reperfusion, did not improve 90-day functional outcome (numerically inferior, acOR 0.60, CI crosses 1 just barely), and was safe regarding sICH. The numerical inferiority on mRS 0-2 (27% vs 38%, acOR 0.60, 95% CI 0.34-1.06) is the more striking finding and should give pause despite formal non-significance - hypothesis-worsening signal. Routine IA tenecteplase for incomplete reperfusion is not supported.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: anterior-circulation incomplete reperfusion (eTICI 2a-2c) after EVT; I: 3 mg IA tenecteplase + BMT; C: BMT alone; O: early angiographic reperfusion at 25 min and complete reperfusion at 24 h.
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: Multicentre, randomised, open-label, blinded-endpoint - appropriate for an angiographic primary endpoint with adjudication on imaging.
critical_appraisal.population_imaging_gate_and_external_val: eTICI 2a-2c is a sizeable subset of EVT cases; results externally valid to the partial recanalisation scenario.
critical_appraisal.treatment_contrast_and_contamination: BMT comparator; if BMT included intensive antiplatelet/antithrombotic regimens that masked TNK benefit, this could attenuate.
critical_appraisal.endpoint_validity_and_interpretability: Reperfusion improvement at 25 min interpretable but proximal to clinical outcomes. The 90-d mRS data are clinically meaningful and do not favour TNK.
critical_appraisal.statistical_validity: Adjusted RDs with CIs spanning zero are unambiguous null on angiographic endpoints. The 90-d mRS acOR 0.60 (CI 0.34-1.06) - point estimate clearly worse, CI crosses 1 narrowly - should not be reported as 'safe' alone.
critical_appraisal.safety_signal_interpretation: sICH 5.2% vs 8.6% - numerically lower with TNK, possibly chance. The relevant safety concern is the directionally worse 90-d mRS, not bleeding.
critical_appraisal.practice_impact: Negative - PI explicitly stated routine IA tenecteplase as adjunct for incomplete reperfusion is not supported.
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Full mRS shift analysis, heterogeneity by eTICI grade, full safety dataset including parenchymal haemorrhage rates.

confidence.reason: Numerical results sourced verbatim from official ESO Day-1 press release.

studies.full_title: AVERT DOSE: Phase III, multi-arm, adaptive randomised trial to determine optimal early (<48 hr start) mobility training in patients with mild and moderate ischaemic stroke
studies.all_authors: Bernhardt J, et al. (The Florey Institute, Melbourne)

concurrent_publication.journal: N/A - main results manuscript not yet indexed

methodology.design: Phase III, multi-arm, adaptive randomised trial
methodology.randomization: 4 arms - different mobility regimens, all initiated <48 h
methodology.enrollment_dates: Stopped early due to funding constraints

population.inclusion_criteria: Mild and moderate ischaemic stroke; able to start mobility training <48 h

arms.intervention: 4 mobility training regimens (functional, task-specific, upright movement) by physio/nurse

primary_endpoint.definition: Functional recovery at 3 months (presented separately for mild vs moderate stroke)

primary_metrics.raw_events: N/A - specific numerical results not in press release

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: Largest acute-stroke mobility-dose-finding RCT (n=1,000 across 7 countries, 50 hospitals). Day-1 ESO press release confirms presented but does NOT report numerical primary outcome data. Until full results, do not change current practice.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: mild and moderate ischaemic stroke <48 h; I/C: 4 mobility regimens; O: 3-month functional recovery.
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: Multi-arm adaptive Bayesian appropriate for dose-finding. Early stop for funding rather than futility/efficacy is a key threat to internal validity.
critical_appraisal.population_imaging_gate_and_external_val: Mild/moderate stroke is largest stroke population - strong external validity.
critical_appraisal.treatment_contrast_and_contamination: Multi-arm contamination risk; protocol details on training/separation are key.
critical_appraisal.safety_signal_interpretation: Mortality and adverse events not yet reported.
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Full numerical results stratified by stroke severity, reasons for trial stop, adverse event data.

confidence.reason: Trial confirmed presented but no numerical outcome data in press release.

studies.full_title: Adjunctive Intra-Arterial Hypothermia With Endovascular Thrombectomy for Acute Anterior Circulation Stroke (CHILL-ART)
studies.all_authors: Huang Z-X, et al. (26 comprehensive stroke centres in China)

concurrent_publication.journal: Protocol in Stroke: Vascular and Interventional Neurology

methodology.randomization: 1:1 to thrombectomy + IA cold-saline vs thrombectomy + room-temperature saline (sham)

population.inclusion_criteria: Anterior-circulation LVO ischaemic stroke, age 18-85, treated <24 h

safety.other_safety_signals: No significant increase in sICH or 90-d mortality in cooling arm

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: First multicentre RCT to show that adding intra-arterial cold-saline cooling to EVT significantly increases 90-day functional independence (54.7% vs 39.8%; aRR 1.36, 95% CI 1.05-1.76; p=0.018) without increasing sICH or mortality. Two cautions: (1) China-only single-region cohort; (2) the companion FOCUS trial in 12 Chinese centres did NOT show functional benefit. Contradictory primary-efficacy results across the two cooling trials need reconciliation.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: anterior LVO 18-85 within 24 h; I: IA cold-saline + EVT; C: IA room-temp saline + EVT; O: mRS 0-2 at 90 days.
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: Multicentre RCT with sham control - strong design preserving blinding. ITT analysis. n=262 modest but sufficient given large effect.
critical_appraisal.population_imaging_gate_and_external_val: Single-country (China) multicentre - generalisability to non-Chinese populations is the principal concern.
critical_appraisal.treatment_contrast_and_contamination: Cold-saline vs room-temperature saline is clean contrast. Cooling temperature, volume, infusion duration not in press summary.
critical_appraisal.endpoint_validity_and_interpretability: mRS 0-2 at 90 days canonical. Adjusted RR 1.36 with CI excluding 1 is robust.
critical_appraisal.statistical_validity: p=0.018, 95% CI 1.05-1.76 - credible signal but lower bound just above 1 (could be fragile). Discrepancy with FOCUS warrants meta-analysis.
critical_appraisal.safety_signal_interpretation: sICH 7.0% vs 9.0% - favourable point estimate, NS. Mortality 13.3% vs 18.0% - favourable, NS.
critical_appraisal.practice_impact: Near-practice-changing pending FOCUS reconciliation and Western validation.
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Reconciliation with FOCUS, Western/multinational replication, full publication with cooling-protocol details, MRI imaging endpoints.

confidence.reason: Numerical results sourced verbatim from official ESO Day-1 press release.

studies.full_title: Selective Intra-Arterial Cooling Infusion With Endovascular Thrombectomy for Acute Ischaemic Stroke
studies.all_authors: Li S, et al. (Beijing Shijitan Hospital, Capital Medical University)

methodology.randomization: 1:1 to selective IA cooling + EVT vs EVT alone

secondary_metrics.result: Adjusted RD -0.174 (95% CI -0.288 to -0.059; p=0.003) favouring cooling

safety.other_safety_signals: Marked reduction in any 24-h ICH (aRD -17.4 pp, p=0.003) - potential BBB protection

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: Negative twin of CHILL-ART: same intervention concept, same country, similar size (n=258 vs 262), but FOCUS shows NO functional benefit (acOR 1.16, p=0.51) while CHILL-ART shows clear functional benefit. FOCUS does corroborate strong safety signal - significantly less 24-h ICH (aRD -17.4 pp, p=0.003), suggesting BBB protection. Split primary-efficacy results means IA-cooling is not yet ready for guideline change.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: anterior LVO <24 h; I: selective IA cooling + EVT; C: EVT alone; O: 90-d mRS shift.
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: Multicentre RCT - internal validity adequate. FOCUS lacked sham control unlike CHILL-ART.
critical_appraisal.population_imaging_gate_and_external_val: Single-country, fewer centres than CHILL-ART (12 vs 26).
critical_appraisal.treatment_contrast_and_contamination: Lack of sham means peri-procedural fluid volume differs - could complicate interpretation.
critical_appraisal.endpoint_validity_and_interpretability: mRS shift canonical; acOR 1.16 (0.75-1.79) is non-significant numerical advantage consistent with no clinically meaningful effect.
critical_appraisal.statistical_validity: Wide CI reflects modest power; ICH benefit is highly significant (p=0.003) and clinically meaningful.
critical_appraisal.safety_signal_interpretation: Strong reduction in any 24-h ICH supports BBB-protective mechanism.
critical_appraisal.practice_impact: Negative for primary efficacy, positive for ICH safety. Combined with CHILL-ART, cooling may protect BBB but functional benefit is uncertain.
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: IPD meta-analysis of CHILL-ART + FOCUS, replication outside China, standardisation of cooling protocol.

confidence.reason: Numerical results sourced verbatim from official ESO Day-1 press release.

studies.full_title: Carotid Artery Stenting During Endovascular Treatment of Acute Ischaemic Stroke (CASES)
studies.all_authors: Uyttenboogaart M, et al. (BeNeFIT consortium; ZonMw and KCE Trials funded)
studies.conference_section: Day 2 LCT - Expanding the Frontiers of Thrombectomy
studies.stroke_subtype: Acute ischaemic stroke with tandem lesion (intracranial LVO + extracranial carotid stenosis >=50% or occlusion)
studies.intervention_domain: Immediate vs deferred carotid artery stenting during EVT

methodology.design: Open-label, multicentre, international RCT with non-inferiority framework
methodology.randomization: 1:1 to immediate CAS at time of EVT vs deferred treatment (CEA, delayed CAS, or BMM)
methodology.centers_countries: 26 centres in the Netherlands and Belgium (BeNeFIT)
methodology.analysis_population: Per-protocol set (PP) and full-analysis set (FAS, including crossovers)

population.inclusion_criteria: Acute ischaemic stroke with tandem lesion, candidates for EVT

arms.intervention: Immediate CAS at time of EVT
arms.control: Deferred treatment (CEA, delayed CAS, or BMM)

primary_endpoint.definition: Modified Rankin Scale at 90 days (ordinal shift) - non-inferiority

primary_metrics.relative_effect_size: FAS acOR 1.06 (95% CI 0.79-1.43) - non-inferiority NOT met; PP acOR 1.10 (95% CI 0.81-1.50) - non-inferior

safety.other_safety_signals: No safety concern raised; numerical advantages favour immediate CAS

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: Largest dedicated tandem-lesion EVT trial. Mixed result: immediate CAS during EVT is NON-INFERIOR to deferred in PP (acOR 1.10, 95% CI 0.81-1.50) but FAILS non-inferiority in FAS (acOR 1.06, 95% CI 0.79-1.43, lower CI bound below 0.80 NI margin). Safety favourable for immediate CAS (sICH 2.1% vs 3.8%, mortality 21% vs 25% - both NS but directionally favourable). PP-vs-FAS divergence: in NI trials, ITT/FAS is the conservative analysis, and its failure is the clinically authoritative conclusion. Does NOT establish non-inferiority in routine practice but provides safety reassurance.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: tandem-lesion AIS eligible for EVT; I: immediate CAS during EVT; C: deferred (CEA, delayed CAS, BMM); O: non-inferiority on 90-d mRS shift, NI margin acOR 0.80.
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: Multicentre RCT with deferred consent, well-powered (n=597), PP and FAS analyses pre-specified. Internal validity strong.
critical_appraisal.population_imaging_gate_and_external_val: Netherlands/Belgium-only - high-quality EVT systems; external validity to lower-volume systems uncertain.
critical_appraisal.treatment_contrast_and_contamination: Crossovers in deferred group received CAS later - PP excludes crossovers, increasing sensitivity to immediate-CAS effect.
critical_appraisal.endpoint_validity_and_interpretability: mRS shift at 90 d canonical. acOR 1.06 (FAS) - lower CI 0.79 below NI margin 0.80 = borderline failure. FAS is the conservative answer.
critical_appraisal.statistical_validity: Pre-specified PP and FAS. NI margin 0.80 reasonable. PP-vs-FAS divergence reflects crossovers diluting immediate-CAS advantage. 0.55 OR for sICH (0.29-1.40) reassuring directionally.

bias_threats.crossover_protocol_violations: Crossovers from deferred to immediate CAS occurred

critical_appraisal.safety_signal_interpretation: sICH 2.1% vs 3.8%, OR 0.55 (0.29-1.40); mortality 21% vs 25%, acOR 0.79 (0.54-1.14) - directionally favourable but NS.
critical_appraisal.practice_impact: Near-practice-changing: safety reassurance supports clinician choice; non-inferiority NOT established in ITT/FAS, individualised decision-making remains standard.
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Subgroup data by occlusion pattern, antiplatelet regimen, time-to-EVT, re-occlusion rates at 90 d, full publication, replication in non-Dutch/Belgian systems.

missing_fields.field: Specific mRS shift distribution per arm

confidence.reason: Numerical results sourced verbatim from official ESO Day-2 press release.

studies.full_title: Cardiovascular Risk Reduction in Patients With Atrial Fibrillation Trial (CRAFT): Effects of Intensive Blood Pressure Control
studies.all_authors: Anderson C, et al. (international Asia-Pacific consortium)
studies.intervention_domain: Intensive vs standard home blood pressure control in AF

concurrent_publication.journal: N/A - main results manuscript not yet indexed

methodology.design: Investigator-initiated, international, multicentre, open-label, parallel-group, blinded-endpoint RCT (PROBE)
methodology.randomization: 1:1 to intensive home SBP <120 mmHg vs standard <135 mmHg, after 2-week run-in

population.inclusion_criteria: Adults >=18 with paroxysmal or persistent AF and >=1 additional CV risk factor

primary_endpoint.definition: Hierarchical composite of CV death, stroke, MI, HF hospitalisation - win-ratio

primary_metrics.raw_events: Intensive vs Standard: 27 vs 33 CV deaths; 70 vs 54 strokes; 16 vs 4 MIs; 104 vs 137 HF hospitalisations
primary_metrics.relative_effect_size: Win ratio 1.02 (95% CI 0.90-1.15; p=0.76); time-to-first-event HR 0.97 (95% CI 0.76-1.25)
primary_metrics.mean_or_median_difference: 8.3 mmHg lower SBP in intensive arm

secondary_metrics.result: Highly significant heterogeneity by sex and age - benefit in younger adults and males

safety.other_safety_signals: Numerically more strokes (70 vs 54) and MIs (16 vs 4) in intensive arm - counterintuitive

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: Clean negative trial of intensive (home SBP <120) vs standard (<135) BP control in AF: win ratio 1.02, p=0.76. The 8.3 mmHg achieved separation was substantial. The numerical pattern is concerning - fewer CV deaths and HF hospitalisations with intensive treatment, but MORE strokes (70 vs 54) and MORE MIs (16 vs 4). The MI imbalance suggests potential J-curve effect of aggressive BP lowering. Subgroup heterogeneity framing should not drive practice change without prospective replication.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: AF patients with >=1 CV risk factor; I: home SBP <120; C: home SBP <135; O: hierarchical CV death/stroke/MI/HF.
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: PROBE adequate power (1,676 patients, 2.5-y follow-up). Run-in phase reduces non-adherence bias. Hierarchical win-ratio sensitive to event ordering - HF dominated.
critical_appraisal.population_imaging_gate_and_external_val: China/Japan-only - generalisability to Western AF populations uncertain.
critical_appraisal.treatment_contrast_and_contamination: 8.3 mmHg achieved separation confirms protocol adherence.
critical_appraisal.endpoint_validity_and_interpretability: Hierarchical composite with HF last - HF events dominate. The numerical stroke increase (70 vs 54) is most worrisome for stroke neurologists.
critical_appraisal.statistical_validity: p=0.76, CI 0.90-1.15 - tight CI excluding meaningful effect either way.
critical_appraisal.safety_signal_interpretation: Identical SAE rates; higher MI count in intensive arm warrants investigation.
critical_appraisal.practice_impact: Negative - does NOT support intensive home SBP <120 in AF for primary CV prevention.
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Subgroup heterogeneity p-values, safety analysis by age/sex strata, generalisability to Western patients, full publication.

missing_fields.missing_reason: Press release reports heterogeneity 'highly significant' but no specific p

missing_fields.field: Achieved BP in each arm (final mean)
Specific stroke type counts (ischemic vs ICH)

confidence.reason: Numerical results sourced verbatim from official ESO Day-2 press release.

studies.full_title: Confirming the Mechanism of Motivational Interviewing Therapy After Stroke (COMMITS)
studies.conference_section: Day 2 LCT - Recovery and Secondary Prevention

methodology.randomization: 1:1:1 to MIBI + usual care vs attention control + usual care vs usual care alone
methodology.blinding: Outcome assessor blinded; intervention not blindable
methodology.centers_countries: 16 stroke units in the United Kingdom
methodology.superiority_or_noninferiority_framework: Superiority on depressive symptoms at 3 months

powering.statistical_assumptions: ANCOVA with baseline depression score adjustment

population.inclusion_criteria: Stroke patients within 12 weeks of event

arms.intervention: MIBI alongside usual care - 4 structured remote sessions by trained staff
arms.control: Attention control with similar contact time; or usual care alone

primary_metrics.raw_events: N/A - primary outcome data NOT reported in press release

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: Large UK 3-arm RCT of motivational-interviewing depression prevention after stroke. Day-2 press release confirms presentation but does NOT report numerical outcome data. The 3-arm design (MIBI vs attention control vs usual care) is methodologically strong because it dissociates 'specific MIBI effect' from 'non-specific contact time effect'.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: stroke patients within 12 weeks; I: MIBI + usual care; C: attention control or usual care; O: depressive symptoms at 3 months.
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: 3-arm RCT with attention control - methodologically optimal. 16 UK units. Engagement reportedly high.
critical_appraisal.treatment_contrast_and_contamination: Attention control with similar contact time isolates the active mechanism.
critical_appraisal.statistical_validity: Power adequate for moderate effect at n=1,246.
critical_appraisal.practice_impact: Pending - could inform NICE/UK stroke guideline if positive.
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Full numerical primary outcome results across 3 arms, cost-effectiveness data, generalisability.

confidence.reason: Trial confirmed presented but no numerical primary outcome results in press release.

studies.full_title: Distal Ischaemic Stroke Treatment with Adjustable Low-profile Stent retriever (DISTALS) - Tigertriever 13 in Distal/Medium-Vessel Occlusions
studies.all_authors: Investigators per Endovascular Today / BusinessWire press release
studies.conference_section: Day 2 LCT - Expanding the Frontiers of Thrombectomy
studies.stroke_subtype: Distal/medium-vessel occlusion (DMVO) ischaemic stroke ineligible for IV thrombolysis
studies.intervention_domain: Mechanical thrombectomy with low-profile stent retriever (Tigertriever 13)

methodology.randomization: Tigertriever 13 thrombectomy vs medical management alone

population.inclusion_criteria: DMVO acute ischaemic stroke, ineligible for IV thrombolysis, within 24 h

primary_endpoint.definition: Successful reperfusion at 24 h (CT perfusion-defined) without sICH

safety.other_safety_signals: Zero sICH in intervention arm - strong safety signal

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: Small (n=149) but stunning device trial: Tigertriever 13 achieved successful reperfusion in 86.3% vs 27.7% in medical management with 0% sICH. Huge effect size and zero sICH support device's mechanical efficacy in DMVO. HOWEVER: broader DMVO thrombectomy literature (DISTAL, ESCAPE-MeVO) has shown NO functional benefit for routine MeVO thrombectomy despite excellent reperfusion. DISTALS readout is reperfusion endpoint, NOT 90-day functional outcome. Device should be viewed as enabling - not justifying - distal thrombectomy.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: DMVO AIS ineligible for IV thrombolysis <24 h; I: Tigertriever 13; C: medical management; O: 24-h reperfusion without sICH.
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: Multicentre RCT - small sample (n=149). Open-label thrombectomy is unavoidable.
critical_appraisal.population_imaging_gate_and_external_val: DMVO heterogeneous (M2/M3, ACA, PCA branches); occlusion-segment distribution not specified.
critical_appraisal.treatment_contrast_and_contamination: Comparator is medical management alone - strong contrast.
critical_appraisal.endpoint_validity_and_interpretability: 24-h reperfusion is process measure, not patient-relevant. Tells us nothing definitive about disability.
critical_appraisal.statistical_validity: p<0.001 with such large absolute difference is statistically robust.
critical_appraisal.safety_signal_interpretation: 0% sICH in intervention arm exceptional but small-sample.
critical_appraisal.practice_impact: Near-practice-changing for device approval; functional-outcome data needed before clinical practice change.
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: 90-day mRS distribution and shift OR with 95% CI, cost-effectiveness, subgroup data by occlusion segment, reconciliation with DISTAL/ESCAPE-MeVO.

missing_fields.field: Specific OR with 95% CI for primary endpoint

confidence.reason: Topline numerical reperfusion result widely reported via Endovascular Today and BusinessWire.

studies.full_title: Endovascular Thrombectomy for Large Ischaemic Core Stroke: An IPD Meta-Analysis (ATLAS Collaboration) - RESCUE-Japan LIMIT, ANGEL-ASPECT, SELECT2, TENSION, TESLA, LASTE
studies.conference_section: Day 2 LCT - Expanding the Frontiers of Thrombectomy
studies.stroke_subtype: Anterior-circulation LVO ischaemic stroke with large infarct core

concurrent_publication.journal: N/A - ESOC 2026-specific manuscript DOI not yet indexed

methodology.design: IPD meta-analysis of 6 randomised trials (RESCUE-Japan LIMIT, ANGEL-ASPECT, SELECT2, TENSION, TESLA, LASTE)

population.inclusion_criteria: Anterior-circulation LVO presenting <24 h with large infarct core

primary_metrics.relative_effect_size: Adjusted OR for mRS shift 1.63 (95% CI 1.42-1.87); p<0.001; aRR 2.61 for mRS 0-2; p<0.001

secondary_metrics.result: Benefit consistent across age, sex, NIHSS, time-to-treatment, stroke size, mismatch profile

safety.other_safety_signals: Net benefit of EVT not outweighed by sICH risk per ATLAS authors

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: Definitive IPD meta-analysis for large-core thrombectomy: 1,886 patients across 6 RCTs, mRS shift OR 1.63 (1.42-1.87), p<0.001, with consistent benefit across age, sex, NIHSS, time-to-treatment, stroke size, and mismatch profile. Consolidates the post-2022 paradigm shift and resolves residual uncertainty about subgroup heterogeneity. Absolute mRS 0-2 rates (9.5% vs 7.5%) remain low but the relative benefit is substantial.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: anterior LVO with large infarct core; I: EVT + standard medical care; C: standard medical care alone; O: 90-d mRS shift.
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: IPD meta-analysis of 6 RCTs with consistent inclusion criteria - strong internal validity. Broad geographic representation.
critical_appraisal.population_imaging_gate_and_external_val: Large-core operationalised differently across trials; IPD harmonisation addresses this.
critical_appraisal.treatment_contrast_and_contamination: Crossovers in some parent trials addressed in IPD.
critical_appraisal.endpoint_validity_and_interpretability: mRS shift canonical; aOR 1.63 with tight CI is robust.
critical_appraisal.statistical_validity: Pre-specified mixed-effects model, large sample, tight CIs.

bias_threats.crossover_protocol_violations: Crossovers in SELECT2 noted; addressed in IPD

critical_appraisal.safety_signal_interpretation: sICH higher with EVT but offset by mortality reduction; net benefit positive.
critical_appraisal.practice_impact: Practice-confirming and consolidating - supports broad EVT application across the large-core spectrum.
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Already practice-aligned for most centres; IPD provides definitive subgroup data for marginal cases.

confidence.reason: Numerical effect estimates from Medscape and Neuro News (corroborated); ESOC-2026-specific manuscript DOI not yet indexed.

studies.full_title: Triple Therapy Prevention of Recurrent Intracerebral Disease EveNts Trial (TRIDENT): low-dose triple antihypertensive pill vs placebo after spontaneous ICH
studies.lead_author: George Medicines / TRIDENT investigators (Anderson, Delcourt, et al.)
studies.conference_section: Concurrent publication referenced at ESOC 2026 (cognitive substudy in late-breakers)

methodology.randomization: 1:1 to triple-pill (telmisartan 20 mg + indapamide 1.25 mg + amlodipine 2.5 mg) vs placebo

population.inclusion_criteria: Clinically stable patients with spontaneous ICH and elevated BP needing pharmacological control

imaging_parameters.imaging_modality: Per local standard for ICH diagnosis

arms.intervention: Triple-pill: telmisartan 20 mg + indapamide 1.25 mg + amlodipine 2.5 mg once daily

primary_metrics.raw_events: N/A specific event counts in indexed press
primary_metrics.relative_effect_size: ~40% relative risk reduction in recurrent stroke
primary_metrics.mean_or_median_difference: Mean SBP reduction ~9 mmHg with triple-pill vs placebo

secondary_metrics.result: N/A - specific cognitive numerical results not in indexed ESOC 2026 press release

sICH.rate: No excess of intracranial haemorrhage with triple-pill

safety.other_safety_signals: No excess hypotension or syncope reported

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: Definitive trial for post-ICH BP control: low-dose triple-pill cuts recurrent stroke ~40% with mean SBP reduction of 9 mmHg and no excess ICH. Published in NEJM 22 Apr 2026. ESOC 2026 cognitive substudy (LB128) extends this - specific cognitive numerical results not in indexed press. Parent trial: practice-changing.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: post-acute stable spontaneous-ICH patients; I: low-dose triple-pill; C: placebo; O: recurrent stroke (parent); cognitive decline/dementia (substudy).
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: International double-blind RCT - strong internal validity.
critical_appraisal.treatment_contrast_and_contamination: Mean 9 mmHg SBP separation - clinically meaningful.
critical_appraisal.endpoint_validity_and_interpretability: Recurrent stroke patient-relevant; ~40% RRR with 9 mmHg SBP reduction is consistent with prior evidence.
critical_appraisal.statistical_validity: Per press summary - formal estimates in NEJM publication.
critical_appraisal.safety_signal_interpretation: No excess ICH or other safety concerns.
critical_appraisal.practice_impact: Parent: practice-changing; cognitive substudy: pending full results.
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Cognitive substudy effect sizes, subgroup analyses by ICH location and aetiology, adherence rates, cost data.

missing_fields.missing_reason: Not in indexed ESOC 2026 press release

missing_fields.field: Specific HR with 95% CI for recurrent stroke
missing_fields.missing_reason: Press summarised as ~40% RRR; full estimates in NEJM

confidence.reason: Parent trial data widely reported (NEJM 2026-04-22); cognitive substudy specific results not yet in indexed sources.

studies.full_title: Improving Early Reperfusion With Adjuvant Dornase Alfa in Large Vessel Ischaemic Stroke
studies.intervention_domain: Adjunct intravenous dornase alfa (recombinant human DNase 1)

methodology.randomization: IV dornase alfa 0.125, 0.25, 0.5, or 1 mg/kg vs standard care

population.inclusion_criteria: LVO acute ischaemic stroke eligible for thrombolysis +/- EVT

arms.intervention: IV dornase alfa (multiple dose arms) + standard reperfusion therapy

primary_endpoint.definition: Reperfusion improvement at initial angiogram (eTICI improvement)

primary_metrics.raw_events: N/A - primary numerical results not in indexed sources

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: Bayesian dose-finding Phase 2 trial of IV dornase alfa as a NETs-targeting adjunct to reperfusion. Confirmed presented at ESOC 2026 Day-1 LCT, but no quantitative primary-endpoint results are publicly indexed at extraction time. Assessment deferred until full data release.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: LVO ischaemic stroke; I: IV dornase alfa (dose-finding); C: reperfusion therapy alone; O: reperfusion improvement on initial angiogram.
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Full Phase 2 results with dose-response curves, sICH and mortality.

confidence.reason: Trial confirmed presented at ESOC 2026 per TCTMD preview; numerical primary results not yet in indexed sources.

studies.full_title: Large Artery Occlusion Treated in Late time window with Mechanical Thrombectomy (LATE-MT) - 24-72 h window
studies.conference_section: Day 2 LCT - Expanding the Frontiers of Thrombectomy
studies.stroke_subtype: Anterior-circulation LVO ischaemic stroke, very late window
studies.intervention_domain: Mechanical thrombectomy in 24-72 h time window

methodology.design: Multi-centre, prospective, randomised, open-label, adaptive group-sequential trial (China)
methodology.randomization: 1:1 to EVT + BMT vs BMT alone

population.inclusion_criteria: Anterior-circulation LVO presenting 24-72 h, NIHSS >=6, viable cerebral tissue

imaging_parameters.imaging_modality: Per local standard with viable tissue requirement

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: First dedicated 24-72 h window thrombectomy RCT. Confirmed presented at ESOC 2026 Day-2 LCT, but no numerical primary-endpoint results are publicly indexed at extraction time. If positive, would extend EVT eligibility well beyond the 24-h DAWN/DEFUSE-3 standard. Assessment deferred.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: anterior LVO 24-72 h with NIHSS >=6 and viable tissue; I: EVT + BMT; C: BMT alone; O: 90-d mRS shift.
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Full results, imaging-gate criteria, subgroup data by time window.

confidence.reason: Trial confirmed presented per TCTMD preview and Curtain Raiser; numerical results not in indexed sources.

studies.full_title: Mechanical Thrombectomy for Mild Ischaemic Stroke (MILD-MT) - anterior LVO with low NIHSS
studies.conference_section: Day 2 LCT - Expanding the Frontiers of Thrombectomy
studies.stroke_subtype: Anterior-circulation LVO with mild deficit (low NIHSS)

population.inclusion_criteria: Anterior-circulation LVO with low NIHSS at presentation

arms.crossover_or_rescue_therapy: Rescue EVT permitted in BMT arm if neurological deterioration

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: Addresses the contested EVT-for-low-NIHSS-LVO question. Confirmed presented at ESOC 2026 per TCTMD preview, but no numerical results in indexed Day-2 press release. Assessment deferred.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: anterior LVO with low NIHSS; I: EVT; C: BMT alone; O: 90-d mRS.
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: RCT - rescue-EVT in BMT arm is the central interpretation challenge
critical_appraisal.population_imaging_gate_and_external_val: Single-country China; cut-off NIHSS not in indexed sources
critical_appraisal.treatment_contrast_and_contamination: Rescue EVT in BMT arm dilutes treatment contrast
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Full results, NIHSS cut-off, rescue-EVT crossover rate.

confidence.reason: Trial confirmed presented per TCTMD preview; numerical results not yet indexed.

studies.full_title: Extending the Time Window for Intravenous Tenecteplase in Patients With Distal Medium Vessel Occlusion Stroke (TNK-MeVO)
studies.conference_section: Day 3 LCT - Rare Targets and New Molecules
studies.stroke_subtype: Distal/medium-vessel occlusion ischaemic stroke, extended time window
studies.intervention_domain: IV tenecteplase 0.25 mg/kg in 4.5-24 h window for DMVO

methodology.randomization: 1:1 to IV TNK 0.25 mg/kg vs placebo within 4.5-24 h

population.inclusion_criteria: DMVO ischaemic stroke within 4.5-24 h; imaging eligibility = <50% ischaemic core in affected territory at >=6 h

imaging_parameters.core_volume_limit: <50% ischaemic core in affected territory at >=6 h

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: Would be a meaningful expansion of thrombolysis eligibility if positive, addressing the wake-up/late-presenting DMVO population currently without proven reperfusion options. Confirmed presented at ESOC 2026 Day-3 per Curtain Raiser; Day-3 ESO press release not yet posted at extraction time.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: DMVO patients 4.5-24 h with viable cerebral tissue; I: IV TNK 0.25 mg/kg; C: placebo; O: 90-d mRS shift.
critical_appraisal.population_imaging_gate_and_external_val: Imaging-gate trial; generalisability requires Western imaging-protocol harmonisation
critical_appraisal.safety_signal_interpretation: Pending - particular attention to sICH given extended window
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Full numerical results, sICH rate, MRI/CTP gate adherence, baseline NIHSS distribution.

confidence.reason: Trial confirmed presented per TCTMD preview and Curtain Raiser; Day-3 numerical results not in indexed sources.

studies.full_title: Rising Stroke Rates Highlight Widening Ethnic and Socioeconomic Inequalities - South London Stroke Register, 30-year cohort
studies.all_authors: Pantoja-Ruiz et al. (King's College London / SLSR consortium)
studies.intervention_domain: Epidemiology - stroke incidence trends and ethnic/socioeconomic disparities

population.inclusion_criteria: All first-ever strokes among South London catchment population

primary_endpoint.definition: Stroke incidence rate over time, stratified by ethnicity and SES

primary_metrics.rate: Stroke incidence rose 13% in 2020-2024 after a 34% prior decline
primary_metrics.relative_effect_size: IRR 2.31 Black African vs White; IRR 2.00 Black Caribbean vs White

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: Important population-level signal: after three decades of declining stroke incidence, South London shows a 13% RISE in 2020-2024, with stark ethnic disparities - Black African (IRR 2.31) and Black Caribbean (IRR 2.00) populations bear the largest burden. Post-pandemic timing suggests COVID-era care disruption, persistent untreated hypertension, widening socioeconomic inequalities are reversing prior gains. Clinical-epidemiology call to action: target screening, intensive secondary prevention, access in disadvantaged groups.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: South London population (~333,000); E: ethnic and socioeconomic exposure; O: stroke incidence rate ratios over 30 years.
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: Prospective population-based registry - gold standard with verified case ascertainment
critical_appraisal.population_imaging_gate_and_external_val: South London geographically defined and ethnically diverse - strong external validity for urban UK populations
critical_appraisal.endpoint_validity_and_interpretability: Adjudicated stroke incidence rate ratios robust
critical_appraisal.statistical_validity: Poisson regression with 30 years person-time provides high precision; specific 95% CIs not in press summary
critical_appraisal.practice_impact: Hypothesis-generating for clinical care; clear policy implication
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Subtype-specific incidence, age-standardised rates, 95% CIs for IRRs, parallel data from other UK regions.

confidence.reason: Numerical results sourced verbatim from official ESO press release.

studies.study_acronym: Penn et al. - U-shaped heart rate-stroke association
studies.full_title: Both Very Low and Very High Heart Rates Are Significantly Associated With Stroke Risk - UK Biobank cohort analysis
studies.all_authors: Penn D, Gill F, Warrington O, Webb A
studies.intervention_domain: Epidemiology - resting heart rate and stroke risk

population.inclusion_criteria: UK Biobank participants with baseline resting HR measurement

primary_endpoint.definition: Incident stroke as a function of resting heart rate

primary_metrics.rate: U-shaped: nadir 60-69 bpm; +25% risk <50 bpm; +45% risk >=90 bpm
primary_metrics.relative_effect_size: +25% risk for HR <50 bpm; +45% risk for HR >=90 bpm vs nadir 60-69 bpm

critical_appraisal.bottom_line_for_vascular_neurologist: UK Biobank confirms a U-shaped relationship between resting HR and 14-year stroke risk (nadir 60-69 bpm, +25% at <50 bpm, +45% at >=90 bpm). High-HR association is well-established (autonomic dysfunction, AF, sympathetic overactivity); low-HR signal requires careful interpretation - patients on rate-controlling drugs may have low resting HR for protective reasons, creating confounding by indication. Without medication adjustment, the bradycardia signal could reflect underlying disease rather than HR per se.
critical_appraisal.pico_and_clinical_question: P: UK Biobank participants 40-69 at baseline; E: resting heart rate; O: incident stroke over 14 years.
critical_appraisal.design_and_internal_validity: Large prospective cohort with adjudicated stroke endpoints - strong internal validity for association but cannot establish causality
critical_appraisal.population_imaging_gate_and_external_val: UK only, mid-life baseline; generalisability uncertain
critical_appraisal.endpoint_validity_and_interpretability: Adjudicated stroke; HR measurement once at baseline (single-time exposure misclassification possible)
critical_appraisal.statistical_validity: n=460,000 with 12,290 events provides ample precision; specific 95% CIs not in press summary

bias_threats.selection_bias: UK Biobank healthy-volunteer bias (~5% participation rate)

critical_appraisal.practice_impact: Hypothesis-generating - identifies HR as risk-stratification marker, not therapeutic target
critical_appraisal.what_i_would_need_before_changing_practi: Adjustment for rate-controlling medications, AF status interaction, confounding-by-indication analysis, replication in non-UK populations.

confidence.reason: Headline numerical results from official press release; statistical detail pending full publication.
